title: Hypervigilance

nodes.pattern: Staying alert and scanning for what might go wrong
nodes.emotion: An underlying sense of unease, even when things are calm
nodes.adaptation: Learning to read rooms and predict danger early

future: As safety grows, the alertness can soften into discernment you choose to use, rather than a state you live in.
reflection: Where in your life do you feel safe enough to let your guard down, even a little?

title: People Pleasing

nodes.pattern: Putting others' needs and comfort ahead of your own
nodes.emotion: Fear of disappointing people or causing upset
nodes.adaptation: Keeping the peace to stay connected and safe
nodes.origin: Relationships where love or calm felt conditional

future: Your care for others can stay, with room added for your own needs to count too.
reflection: What would it feel like to say what you want, even if it might disappoint someone?

title: Emotional Suppression

nodes.pattern: Pushing feelings down rather than letting them surface
nodes.emotion: A sense that emotions are unsafe or unwelcome
nodes.adaptation: Containing feelings to stay functional and accepted
nodes.origin: Settings where emotions were dismissed or punished

future: Containment can become a choice rather than a reflex, with space to feel when it's safe.
reflection: When did you last let yourself fully feel something, and what was that like?

title: Difficulty Trusting Others

nodes.pattern: Keeping a part of yourself guarded in relationships
nodes.emotion: An expectation that closeness may lead to hurt
nodes.adaptation: Relying on yourself to avoid being let down
nodes.origin: Early bonds where trust was broken or unreliable

future: Trust can be rebuilt slowly, in relationships that prove safe over time.
reflection: Who in your life has earned a little more of your trust than you usually give?

title: Responsibility Overload

nodes.pattern: Carrying responsibility for people and outcomes beyond your own
nodes.emotion: Guilt or anxiety when you're not holding things together
nodes.adaptation: Becoming the dependable one others rely on
nodes.origin: Growing up taking on adult roles before you were ready

future: Responsibility can be shared, and rest can become something you're allowed to have.
reflection: What is one thing you carry that isn't actually yours to hold?

title: Conflict Avoidance

nodes.pattern: Steering away from friction or difficult conversations
nodes.adaptation: Avoiding conflict to keep relationships stable
nodes.origin: Homes where conflict felt dangerous or destabilizing

future: Conflict can become something you can navigate, not only escape.
reflection: What is a small thing you've been avoiding saying to someone?

title: Achievement-Based Self-Worth

nodes.pattern: Measuring your worth by what you accomplish
nodes.emotion: A worry that resting or stopping makes you less valuable
nodes.adaptation: Earning approval and security through achievement
nodes.origin: Environments where being 'enough' depended on performance

future: Your worth can settle into something you simply have, separate from what you produce.
reflection: Who would you be if you achieved nothing this week — and is that person still worthy of care?

title: Difficulty Resting

nodes.pattern: Struggling to slow down or switch off
nodes.adaptation: Staying busy to stay safe, useful, or distracted
nodes.origin: Times when stopping felt unsafe or unaffordable

future: Rest can become a place of safety rather than a threat to manage.
reflection: What does your body do when you finally allow it to rest?
